description: At night, a city skyline with illuminated buildings is shown. The setting changes to a sidewalk outside a newsstand. Joey walks briskly toward the stand, followed by Rachel, Monica, Phoebe, and Chandler.

Rachel says, "Joey, just slow down. There's not going to be sold out of papers at 01:00 in the morning." Joey reaches the newsstand, picks up a newspaper, and turns back toward the group, saying, "I'm excited."
Joey says, "I've never gotten reviewed before." Monica steps closer and says, "You were so amazing at the king. I'm really impressed."

Joey smiles and replies, "I was." Monica says, "Although, you know what? You might want to consider wearing underwear next time." She gestures with one hand. "Yeah, because when you sat down on your throne..."
Monica continues, "...you could just kind of see your royal subject." Joey's expression remains steady.
Ross joins the group, holding a newspaper.
Ross says, "No one wore underwear back then. I'm sure the critics appreciated your authenticity." Chandler, standing nearby with a paper, says, "Nice genitals, by the way."

Joey smiles and says, "Thanks, man." He opens his newspaper and says, "Here it is. Here it is." Looking at the page, he reads aloud, "The only thing worse than the mindless adolescent direction is Joseph..."
Joey continues reading, his smile fading: "...Tribbiani's disturbingly, unskilled portrayal of the king."
Joey lowers the newspaper.
Joey says, "Okay, look, that is one guy's opinion, all right?"
Joey turns to Phoebe and says, "Phoebes, read yours."
Phoebe looks at her paper and reads, "Okay. The only thing worse than the mindless adolescent direction..." She stops speaking. Chandler asks, "does anybody have one from a different paper?"
Joey turns his head toward Ross.
Joey says, "Ross, read yours." Ross glances down at his paper and replies, "I don't want to."
Joey gestures toward Ross's newspaper.
Ross reads, "The only enjoyable moment is when the king is beheaded and he can speak no more."
Joey drops his newspaper onto the sidewalk.

Rachel says, "Joey, honey, they don't know what they're talking about." Joey squats down, resting his arms on his knees, and says, "Maybe they do. I've been doing this ten years, and I haven't gotten..."
Joey continues, "...anywhere. There's got to be a reason."
Ross kneels slightly, touches Joey's arm, and says, "Come on, man."
Ross continues, "You're just paying your dues."
Joey shakes his head, gestures outward, and says, "No, it's too hard. It's not worth it. I quit."
The group voices overlapping protests. Monica points to her newspaper.
Monica says, "Wait 1 minute. Wait a minute. I believe this will change your mind. In a mediocre play, Joseph..."
She smiles and continues reading, "...Tribbiani was able to achieve brilliant new levels of..." She flips the page. "...continued on page 153 sucking."
The scene changes to an apartment kitchen. Joey sits at a round table, his head resting in his hand.
Rachel stands behind Joey, holding his head against her stomach and rubbing his temples. Chandler and Phoebe are seated nearby.
Chandler asks, "Well, was there anything else you ever wanted to be?"
Joey replies, "I don't know."
Joey continues, "When I was little I wanted to be a veterinarian. But then I found out you have to put your hands in the cows and stuff."
Chandler presses his lips together and nods slightly. The apartment door opens.
Ross walks in and says, "Hi."
The group replies with overlapping greetings. Monica stands by the counter.
Monica asks, "are you?"
Ross approaches the table and says, "Yeah, yeah, just a tough day at know, stegosaurus fell over, trapped the kid."
Ross gestures toward the room. Phoebe tilts her head toward him.
Ross points at a black jacket draped over a chair and says, "Well, I know this jacket. This is fun."
Ross continues, "Bobby's jacket. Where is he?" Monica smiles. Ross asks, "What? He's here, isn't he?" She replies, "Maybe." Ross says, "Don't toy with me."
Fun Bobby walks out of the hallway holding a portable phone.
Fun Bobby says, "Whoa."
Ross says, "Hey. You've been working, huh?"
Fun Bobby replies, "Not at all."
Ross and Fun Bobby shake hands and embrace.
Ross laughs and says, "I love this guy."
They separate and pat each other on the back.
Ross says, "I was so psyched to hear you're back with my sister." Fun Bobby replies, "You and me both." Turning toward the table, Fun Bobby asks, "Hey, so what I missed? What I missed? Come..."
Fun Bobby continues, "...on." Monica puts her arm around his waist and says, "Oh, we were just trying to make Joey feel better."

Fun Bobby asks, "Hey, do you need me to pick you up?" Joey says, "No, I'm all right, man. Really." Fun Bobby steps closer, saying, "No, I'm picking you up." Joey stands and says, "Hey, no, seriously, I don't need you to pick."
Fun Bobby lifts Joey off the floor by the waist. Joey laughs while dangling in the air.
Fun Bobby sets Joey down. Joey says, "All right, it still works."
Fun Bobby gestures to the others and asks, "Okay, now before I go, does anybody else need to be picked up?"
Chandler, Phoebe, Ross, and Monica raise their hands simultaneously.
Fun Bobby points at them and says, "I'm still going to go."
Fun Bobby walks over and picks up his jacket from the chair.
Monica says, "Okay. I'll see you later, babe."
Fun Bobby takes a sip from a wine glass.
Fun Bobby gestures toward the group and says, "Public display of affection coming up. You avert your eyes."
Fun Bobby and Monica kiss. Chandler turns away and places his hand over Joey's eyes.

In an apartment living room, Monica stands by the open door as Fun Bobby steps into the hallway. "See you," Fun Bobby says. Monica and the others say their goodbyes. Fun Bobby leaves, and Monica closes the door.
Audience laughter is audible as Monica turns back toward the living room. Ross stands behind the couch where Phoebe is seated. Joey, Chandler, and Rachel sit at a round dining table.
Monica walks toward the kitchen area, gesturing with her hands. "Fun Bobby is so great. Oh, isn't he?" she says.

Monica stands near the kitchen counter. "You know what? I really think this time it may work with him. I mean, he just makes me feel so good. And I've been feeling so" she says, clasping her hands and moving them up and down.
"lousy last couple of months. No job, no boyfriend. Well, at least my cup is half full," Monica continues, raising her index finger.
"Half full of love," Phoebe says from the couch, nodding and smiling.
Audience laughter is heard. The group at the table smiles, and Joey touches his forehead. Phoebe looks at Monica.
"And for our two week anniversary, he's going to take me to his cousin's cabin for the weekend," Monica says, holding her hands together.
"Cabin of love," Phoebe says, tilting her head.
Rachel stands up from the table holding two empty wine bottles and walks toward the counter. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel places the bottles on the counter. "We went through a lot of wine tonight, you guys," she says. Ross steps up behind Monica and places his hands on her shoulders.
Monica turns her head to look at Ross. "Really? I only had two glasses," she says.
"I just had a glass," Joey says from the table, gesturing with his hand.
"Two? I had one glass," Phoebe says from the couch, gesturing with her hand.
Chandler, holding up a mug at the table, says, "I had about a mug full in this lovely I got boned at the Museum of Natural History mug."
Ross, still holding Monica's shoulders, smiles and looks forward as audience laughter is heard.
Rachel holds up two wine bottles, then points to a third. "Okay, so that's what? Two bottles? And yet, somehow we went through five," she says.
The group falls silent. Monica, Joey, Chandler, Phoebe, and Ross exchange glances, their expressions shifting as audience laughter builds.
Joey's eyes widen. He reaches out and touches two of the empty bottles on the counter, staring at them with his mouth slightly open. Audience laughter continues.
Joey's eyes widen further. "Oh," he says over the audience laughter.
"So what? So he drank a lot tonight," Monica says, pacing and gesturing toward the group.
"Yeah, but now that I think about it I don't think I've ever seen Fun Bobby without" Ross says, raising his eyebrows.
"a drink in his" Ross continues, turning his head slightly as he trails off.
Phoebe raises her wine glass and points at it.
"you know. Did you notice how he always starts his stories with okay, I was so wasted. Or oh, we were so bombed?" Phoebe says, gesturing with her glass.
Ross points a finger toward Phoebe, nodding in agreement as audience laughter is heard.
"So I wake up and I'm in this dumpster in Connecticut," Phoebe says, lowering her pitch to imitate Fun Bobby's voice.
Joey leans forward at the table, gesturing with one hand as audience laughter plays.
"Monica, have you ever been with him when he wasn't drinking?" Joey asks, extending both hands outward.
"Well, we just happened to go to a lot of places where you might drink." Monica says, gesturing with her hands.
"I mean, how do you go to a wine tasting without having a drink? Or to a club, or to the" Monica continues, pacing slightly with open hands.
"zoo," Monica finishes, lowering her hands.
The setting changes to a coffee shop. Fun Bobby and Monica sit together on an orange couch. Rachel, wearing an apron, approaches them carrying a tray with mugs.
Monica points to her green mug on the tray. "Rachel, does this have non fat milk?" she asks.
Rachel stands holding the tray, looking down at the mugs.
"I don't know. Why don't you taste it?" Rachel says.
Monica takes a sip from the green mug, swallows, and lowers it, her gaze fixing forward.
"No. Oh, well. Too late. Sorry. You already had some," Rachel says, smiling as she steps back. Audience laughter is heard.
Monica holds the mug. Fun Bobby sits next to her, and Phoebe sits on the arm of the couch holding a pink mug. Fun Bobby reaches into his jacket.
Fun Bobby pulls out a small metal flask. "What do you say we make these coffees Irish," he says, opening the flask.
Rachel looks at them, pointing her finger slightly as Phoebe stands up from the arm of the couch.
"Cake?" Rachel asks, looking toward Phoebe.
"Yeah, we're going to get some cake," Rachel says. She and Phoebe turn and walk away over the audience laughter.
Fun Bobby pours liquid from the flask into his green mug. Monica watches him, her lips pressed together.
"You know, it seems like you've been making an awful lot of stuff Irish lately," Monica says. Fun Bobby continues pouring.
"Well, it would make them Belgian, but the waffles" Fun Bobby says, looking up briefly.
"are hard to get into. That flask, Bobby?" Fun Bobby continues, closing the flask.
Monica looks down briefly, her hands resting in her lap as audience laughter is heard.
"Yeah. Okay, look, maybe this is none of my business or maybe it is, I don't know. But" Monica says, gesturing with one hand.
Fun Bobby turns his head to look at Monica, holding his hands together.
"I'm kind of worried about you," Monica says, maintaining eye contact with him.
"Okay, look, this isn't the first time somebody said something to me about this." Fun Bobby says, gesturing lightly.
"But I don't know, I always made excuses about it. Like I'm" he continues.
"just a social drinker or hey, come on. It's Flag Day," Fun Bobby says, shrugging.
Monica looks at him, her hands clasped, her gaze remaining steady.
"So what are you saying now?" Monica asks.
Fun Bobby looks forward, then turns his head back to Monica.
"I guess I'm saying I'll try and quit." Fun Bobby says.
"I kind of like that you worry about me," he adds, smiling slightly.
Monica smiles and leans in to hug Fun Bobby. Phoebe walks back to the couch holding a slice of cake on a plate.
"So what's going on?" Phoebe asks, sitting down. "I am going to try and quit drinking," Fun Bobby says, keeping one arm around Monica.
"Oh, why?" Phoebe asks, her eyebrows raising.
Fun Bobby and Monica both turn their heads to look at Phoebe. Audience laughter plays as the video ends.
Inside a coffee shop, Phoebe, Monica, and Fun Bobby sit on a couch. Joey and Chandler walk in as audience cheering and applause play. Chandler claps his hands, and Joey says, "Hey,"
Joey and Chandler stop near the couch. The applause fades.
Joey gestures to himself and asks, "guess who's back in show business?" Phoebe, holding a plate with a slice of cake, replies, "Oh, whoa. Lorne Greene?"
Chandler shakes his head. Joey's smile fades as he looks toward Phoebe.
Chandler says to Phoebe, "No, Phoebes. You know why? Because he's dead." Phoebe leans back slightly and says, "Oh, no."
Phoebe lowers her gaze. Chandler gestures toward Joey with one hand.
Chandler says, "Okay, I guess it's going to seem kind of bittersweet now, but Joey, that's who." Joey turns to face the group on the couch.
Phoebe smiles. Joey raises both hands.
Joey says, "Yeah, my agent just called me with an audition for Days of Our Lives." Audience cheering and applause resume.
Fun Bobby holds up a silver flask.
Fun Bobby says, "Here's to Joey."
Fun Bobby extends the flask toward Joey. Joey reaches out to take it.
Fun Bobby says, "Here Joey," as Joey unscrews the cap.
Joey takes a drink from the flask. Phoebe sets her plate down on her lap.
Phoebe gestures with both hands and says, "Oh, God. Well, we have to celebrate. You know what we should do? We should do, like, a soap opera theme."
Chandler points forward and says, "Hey, yeah."
Chandler continues, "We could all sleep together. And then one of us could get amnesia."
Rachel walks over to the table carrying a tray with a cup and a rag. Audience laughter plays.
Chandler looks at Rachel and says, "Hey, Rach, what time do you get off? We're all going to do something tonight." Rachel places the cup on the table.
Rachel picks up the tray, wipes it with the rag, and says, "Well, actually, I'm already done. But I've kind of got plans."
Monica places a hand on her chest.
Monica asks, "You have other friends?"
Rachel looks toward Monica.
Rachel says, "Yeah."
Rachel lowers the tray slightly.
Rachel says, "I have a date."
Phoebe asks, "What? You have a date?" Rachel nods and says, "Yes, I have a date."
Joey asks, "With a man?"
Rachel turns toward Joey. Audience laughter plays.
Rachel says, "No, with a crouton."
Joey looks away from Rachel toward Chandler.
Joey says, "What?" Rachel asks, "What is so strange about me having a date with a crouton?"
Joey sits down in an armchair.
Joey looks at Rachel and says, "Rach."
Joey asks, "What about Ross?"
Rachel looks down.
Joey asks, "Are you still mad at him because he made that list?"
Rachel shakes her head and says, "No, no, I'm not mad at him. I'm not really anything at him anymore."
Monica gestures and asks, "What are you talking about?" Rachel replies, "I don't"
Rachel finishes, "know," and shrugs her shoulders.
Rachel says, "Whatever I was feeling, I'm not." Chandler says, "But you guys came so close."
Rachel crosses her arms and says, "Oh, I know. I'm sorry, you guys."
Rachel continues, "You're just going to have to get used to the fact that I will not be dating Ross."
Rachel turns toward the doorway and clasps her hands together.
A man resembling Ross walks in. Rachel smiles, places a hand on his arm, and says, "Here he is. Hi, guys. This is" The man says, "Hi."
Rachel says, "Russ." Chandler, Monica, and Joey stare at Russ with wide eyes. Russ looks back at them.
The scene changes to the exterior of a building. Text on a window reads, "ESTELLE LEONARD TALENT AGENCY."
The setting changes to an office interior. Estelle sits at a desk, holding a phone receiver to her ear.
Estelle speaks into the phone, "Estelle, Leonard Talent Agency."
Estelle holds a lit cigarette in her hand.
Estelle gestures and says, "Oh, Sammy, would you stop calling me?"
Estelle takes a drag from her cigarette.
Estelle says, "No, no, I'm not saying you're not talented. You're very talented."
Estelle continues, "It's just that with the bird dead and all, there's very little act left."
Joey opens the office door and steps inside.
Estelle waves her free hand and says, "Oh, honey, give me a break."
Estelle looks toward Joey.
Estelle says into the phone, "Oh, I'll talk to you later," and hangs up.
Estelle places her cigarette in an ashtray.
Estelle smiles and asks, "Well, there's my favorite client. So, tell me, darling, how was the audition?"
Joey steps closer and says, "Well, I think it went pretty well. I got a"
Joey continues, "call back for Thursday."
Estelle picks up her cigarette again.
Estelle looks at Joey and asks, "Joey, have you ever seen me ecstatic?"
Joey shakes his head and says, "No." Estelle, maintaining a blank expression, says, "Well, here it is."
Joey forces a smile and chuckles slightly. Audience laughter plays as Joey sits down in a chair opposite the desk.
Joey leans forward and says, "Okay, listen, there's something I want to talk to you about."
Joey adds, "The network casting lady."
Estelle says, "Oh, isn't Lori a?" Joey replies, "Yeah, yeah, she was great."
Joey continues, "But I kind of got the feeling that she was sort"
Joey gestures with his hands and continues, "of coming on to me and I definitely would get the part"
Joey finishes, "if I would have, you know,"
Joey raises his eyebrows and shifts his gaze, leaving the sentence unfinished.
In an office setting, Estelle sits at her desk with Joey seated opposite her.
Joey finishes his sentence, "if I would have sent a little general in." Estelle looks at him with her mouth slightly open, holding a cigarette.
Joey looks at Estelle, who remains still and keeps her gaze on him.
Estelle nods and says, "I see."
Estelle presses buttons on her desk phone, saying, "Well, I'm just going to put in a call here and we'll find out what's going on and straighten it out. Yeah. Hi," as Joey watches her.
Estelle brings her cigarette to her mouth and continues on the phone, "Lori, please."
Estelle pulls a cigarette from a dispenser on her desk while Joey nods slightly.
Estelle speaks into the phone, "Hi, darling. So how about Joey Tribbiani for the part of the cab driver?" Joey smiles.
Estelle asks the person on the phone, "Isn't he terrific?"
Joey's smile fades as he watches Estelle listen to the person on the phone.
Estelle says, "Okay, doll, talk to you later," and hangs up the phone.
Estelle lowers her hand from the receiver.
Estelle points her cigarette at Joey and says, "Yeah, you're gonna have to sleep with"
Joey chuckles, looking down and then back up.
The scene changes to the Central Perk coffee shop. Rachel pours coffee into a mug for Monica at the counter and asks, "oh, what's the matter? Fun Bobby. What, isn't he sober?"
Monica looks down at her mug and says, "Oh, he's sober, all right. Just turns out that Fun Bobby was fun"
Monica continues, "for a reason."
Rachel walks away with a coffee pot. Monica carries two mugs toward a couch where Chandler and Phoebe are sitting, with Bobby seated in an armchair opposite them.
Monica hands a mug to Bobby and says, "All right, here you go, sweetie."
Bobby takes the mug.
Bobby says, "Thanks. You want to hear something funny?"
Chandler leans forward, resting his hands on his knees, and says, "Oh, God, yes."
Bobby looks toward Chandler.
Bobby says, "There are no hardware stores open past midnight in the Village."
Chandler and Phoebe look at Bobby. Monica sits on the edge of the couch next to Chandler.
Smiling slightly, Phoebe says, "That is funny."
Bobby looks at Monica, who stares forward.
Bobby makes circular motions with his hand and says, "I needed to buy a hammer the other night, and I'm out walking around the neighborhood and I'm walking, and I'm walking,"
Bobby continues his hand motions, saying, "and I'm walking and I'm walking."
Chandler clasps his hands together and interrupts, "Okay, so you're saying you were walking, but apparently"
Bobby says, "there are no hardware stores open past midnight in the Village."
Monica continues to stare forward. Chandler and Phoebe keep their eyes on Bobby.
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "So it's a helpful fact as well as funny."
Monica sits silently next to Chandler, her facial expression unchanging.
Monica checks her watch.
Monica looks at Bobby and asks, "Say, honey, don't you have to be at your interview now?" Bobby replies, "Oh, yeah."
Bobby stands up, holding his mug and saucer.
Bobby says, "See you guys."
Chandler and Phoebe say, "Bye."
Bobby turns and walks away.
Chandler stands up, steps behind the couch, places his hands on its back, and says, "Ridiculously dull Bobby."
Monica turns her head to look up at Chandler.
Monica raises her hands slightly and says, "Oh, my God." Phoebe replies, "It's not that bad."
Monica gestures emphatically and says, "Not that bad? Did you not hear the hammer story? Okay,"
Phoebe says, "don't get all Squinky."
Rachel walks up to the group, stopping near Phoebe.
Monica gestures with both hands and says, "Maybe it was just the kind of story where you had to be there. But I'm gonna be there for the rest of my life. Me."
Pressing a hand to her chest, Monica continues, "I mean, I can't break up with him. I'm the one who made him quit drinking."
Monica drops her hands and keeps her mouth slightly open while Rachel and Phoebe listen silently.
The screen turns black.
